## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [ ] New hook (non-breaking)
- [ ] New agent (non-breaking)

## Verification

<!-- How did you test this? Paste the command(s) you ran. -->

- [ ] `shellcheck` passes on changed shell files
- [ ] If a hook was added/modified, manual end-to-end run confirmed it triggers (or doesn't) correctly
- [ ] If a new flag/option was added, it's documented in the README
- [ ] No new dependencies (or: new dependencies are justified in the description)
- [ ] Security-relevant changes are flagged in the description
